key_insight: PI-Splines replace the neural-network field in physics-informed learning with a trainable tensor-product B-spline expansion, keeping residual-based training while adding structure, locality, and analytic smoothness control.
engineering_relevance: This is attractive for PDE-constrained modeling when compact support, interpretable parameters, and strong boundary enforcement matter more than fully general neural representations.
method: Represent the unknown solution directly with B-spline control coefficients instead of a neural network.
Train the coefficients by minimizing physics residuals under the same PINN-style loss and collocation setup.
Impose boundary conditions strongly by fixing compatible boundary control points when the spline space allows it.
evidence_signal: The method was tested on several benchmark PDE problems and compared against standard physics-informed frameworks using matched equations, collocation sets, losses, and optimizers to isolate the representation effect.
watch_item: Performance depends on choosing a spline space compatible with the PDE geometry and boundary conditions, so setup and knot/design choices may limit generality.
paper_title: Trainable Spline Representations for Physics-Informed Learning
